title: The Sun episode: Naked tanning, benefits, skin cancer risk, dangers of sunscreen

chunks.title: Introduction to Heart and Soil Supplements
chunks.summary: The speaker shares a positive review of the Heart and Soil supplements, particularly praising the benefits of desiccated testicles, liver, and blood. He enthusiastically endorses these products as life-changing for his health and fitness.
chunks.transcript: this was the best decision check out this review on whole package from heart and soil supplements from zach h i highly recommend this to all men a ton of my friends have bought whole package after i told them about it i've been working out for 16 years consistently and this is the best i've ever felt one thing i didn't expect was a strength increase i've not only gotten stronger but i'm more shredded than i was in my early 20s i will take this product for the rest of my life how cool is that it's amazing to see people benefiting from desiccated testicle i think it's a very powerful thing check it out at heart and soil supplements whole package has desiccated testicle desiccated liver and desiccated blood those are probably three of my favorite organs you can find

chunks.title: Allergy Relief Through Supplements
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses how certain supplements have helped individuals with allergies, providing an anecdote about a user who no longer needs antihistamines. This highlights the impact of nutritional supplements on overall health.
chunks.transcript: allergies are acting up this time of year listen to this one from johanna h a lifelong zyrtec allegra and claritin user not anymore it's crazy to think that this entire allergy season in north carolina where you can physically see and feel the pollen around you i no longer had to take over the counter antihistamines i've never been a fan of taking medicines and i heard about hardened soil it has been saving it has been my saving grace this season i have no allergy symptoms anymore that is our histamine and immune from hardened soil supplements so check us out at heart and

chunks.title: Understanding Sunlight Benefits
chunks.summary: The podcast focuses on the benefits of sunlight, advising against the use of sunscreen. It elaborates on how sunlight is essential for health and challenges common beliefs about its dangers, examining studies related to sunlight exposure and health outcomes.
chunks.transcript: on this week's podcast i wanted to talk about sunlight because we are going into the summer months and many of you may be going in the sun and i hope to dissuade you from putting on sunscreen and other garbage things like that and i hope to help you understand that the sun has many benefits that you should use it carefully but you should not fear the sun no matter what your skin tone is at baseline i talk about all of that in this podcast i talk about benefits of sunlight i talk about a premise about something being essential for optimal human health but then also within the nutritional or medical community it being vilified that doesn't make sense to me i go into potential side effects of sunscreens negative effects of sunscreens i go into questions regarding melanoma whether this is actually truly a skin associated cancer and a very interesting study regarding melanoma and levels of linoleic acid in your subcutaneous adipose issue which you do not want to miss so enjoy this podcast on sun it's

chunks.title: Sunlight's Role in Human Evolution
chunks.summary: The discussion pivots to the evolutionary significance of sunlight for humans, emphasizing how ancestral diets and exposure to sunlight have shaped human health. The speaker argues for the necessity of sunlight in our lives.
chunks.transcript: i have done on sun to date and i hope that it is a good resource for you guys on to the sun episode ghosts on your balls or i don't know what you call it for ladies but you guys have the idea what is up true seekers welcome to another edition of the fundamental health podcast is now may and i wanted to talk about sunlight because this is something that is on my mind a lot these days here in costa rica we're getting into the rainy season but the days are still getting longer because we're in the northern hemisphere many of you listening to this are also in the northern hemisphere and you are approaching bathing suit beach and bikini season so i thought it was a good time to revisit sunlight and think about sunlight from an evolutionary an ancestral from a nutritional biochemical perspective and really do a whole podcast breaking this down as i see it in may of 2022. i want to start the podcast with a consideration of the benefits of sunlight supplemental vitamin d and ultraviolet light and we'll look at both interventional and observational studies along the way to explore the benefits of sunlight then i will begin to discuss skin cancer because the thesis that i will advance in this podcast is that sun is essential for humans but it's been a critical part of our evolution throughout our existence

chunks.title: Link Between Diet and Skin Cancer
chunks.summary: The speaker introduces the correlation between diet, particularly linoleic acid from seed oils, and the incidence of skin cancers. He argues that metabolic health and diet context are crucial in understanding cancer risk linked to sunlight exposure.

chunks.title: Examining Sun Exposure and Cancer Links
chunks.summary: This segment explores various studies about the relationship between sun exposure and different skin cancers, particularly melanoma. The speaker emphasizes the complexity of the relationship and that sun exposure is not the sole risk factor for skin cancer.

chunks.title: Controversy Surrounding Sunscreens
chunks.summary: The speaker raises concerns about common sunscreen ingredients and their potential harm to human health, citing studies that show increased absorption of these chemicals. He recommends caution with commercial sunscreens.
chunks.transcript: adipose tissue levels of fatty acids to really get a sense of linoleic acid what the author is going to say is that the mean percentage of linoleic acid again this is an 18 carbon polyunsaturated fatty acid found primarily in seed oils corn canola sunflower safflower soybean etc that i have major concerns about being one of the massive one of the most notable drivers of chronic disease in our age okay they're saying the mean percentage of this linoleic acid in the triglycerides of the subcutaneous adipose tissue which they call the pla sat the placat of these subjects was substantially higher than that in a similar group examined in 1975 to 1976 in addition the percentage of polyunsaturated fatty acids was found to be higher in melanoma patients than in the controls the p is less than 0.01 and there were significantly more controls than patients who had a low placat okay they're saying the controls had low levels of linoleic acid and their triglycerides and subcutaneous adipose tissue now in the introduction again i'll get into this paper more in this podcast but i want to give you guys a teaser because i thought it was so fascinating that somebody has actually done the research looking at melanoma patients in their subcutaneous adipose issue for levels of linoleic acid in 1974 mackie described five consecutive patients with melanoma in the leg who were seen in sydney within a 12-week period the patients were questioned by a dietitian who reported that they all had substantially reduced their intake of saturated fat and replaced this with polyunsaturated fat 10 months before the onset of the melanoma it was suggested that the increased intake of polyunsaturated fat may have predisposed these patients to the development of melanoma so we're going to pause there

chunks.title: Potential Risks from Dietary Choices
chunks.summary: The discussion includes insights into how certain dietary components, particularly plant-derived substances like sorrelins, may increase sensitivity to sunlight and contribute to skin cancer risks. The speaker recommends moderation in consuming these foods.
